root_text.an7.43:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā kosambiyaṁ viharati ghositārāme.
root_text.an7.43:1.3: Atha kho āyasmā ānando pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya kosambiṁ piṇḍāya pāvisi.
root_text.an7.43:1.6: Yannūnāhaṁ yena aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ ārāmo tenupasaṅkameyyan”ti.
root_text.an7.43:1.7: Atha kho āyasmā ānando yena aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ ārāmo tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā tehi aññatitthiyehi paribbājakehi saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.an7.43:1.8: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi.
root_text.an7.43:2.1: Tena kho pana samayena tesaṁ aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ sannisinnānaṁ sannipatitānaṁ ayamantarākathā udapādi:
root_text.an7.43:2.2: “yo hi koci, āvuso, dvādasavassāni paripuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ carati, ‘niddaso bhikkhū’ti alaṁvacanāyā”ti.
root_text.an7.43:3.1: Atha kho āyasmā ānando tesaṁ aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ bhāsitaṁ neva abhinandi nappaṭikkosi.
root_text.an7.43:3.4: Atha kho āyasmā ānando kosambiyaṁ piṇḍāya caritvā pacchābhattaṁ piṇḍapātapaṭikkanto yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho āyasmā ānando bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.an7.43:4.1: “Idhāhaṁ, bhante, pubbaṇhasamayaṁ nivāsetvā pattacīvaramādāya kosambiṁ piṇḍāya pāvisiṁ.
root_text.an7.43:4.4: Yannūnāhaṁ yena aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ ārāmo tenupasaṅkameyyan’ti …pe…
root_text.an7.43:4.6: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdiṁ.
root_text.an7.43:5.1: Tena kho pana, bhante, samayena tesaṁ aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ sannisinnānaṁ sannipatitānaṁ ayamantarākathā udapādi:
root_text.an7.43:5.2: ‘yo hi koci, āvuso, dvādasavassāni paripuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ carati, niddaso bhikkhūti alaṁvacanāyā’ti.
root_text.an7.43:5.3: Atha khvāhaṁ, bhante, tesaṁ aññatitthiyānaṁ paribbājakānaṁ bhāsitaṁ neva abhinandiṁ nappaṭikkosiṁ.
root_text.an7.43:5.6: Sakkā nu kho, bhante, imasmiṁ dhammavinaye kevalaṁ vassagaṇanamattena niddaso bhikkhu paññāpetun”ti?
root_text.an7.43:6.1: “Na kho, ānanda, sakkā imasmiṁ dhammavinaye kevalaṁ vassagaṇanamattena niddaso bhikkhu paññāpetuṁ.
root_text.an7.43:6.2: Satta kho imāni, ānanda, niddasavatthūni mayā sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā paveditāni.
root_text.an7.43:7.2: Idhānanda, bhikkhu, saddho hoti, hirīmā hoti, ottappī hoti, bahussuto hoti, āraddhavīriyo hoti, satimā hoti, paññavā hoti.
root_text.an7.43:7.3: Imāni kho, ānanda, satta niddasavatthūni mayā sayaṁ abhiññā sacchikatvā paveditāni.
root_text.an7.43:7.4: Imehi kho, ānanda, sattahi niddasavatthūhi samannāgato bhikkhu dvādasa cepi vassāni paripuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ carati, ‘niddaso bhikkhū’ti alaṁvacanāya;
root_text.an7.43:7.5: catubbīsati cepi vassāni paripuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ carati, ‘niddaso bhikkhū’ti alaṁvacanāya;
root_text.an7.43:7.6: chattiṁsati cepi vassāni paripuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ carati, ‘niddaso bhikkhū’ti alaṁvacanāya, aṭṭhacattārīsañcepi vassāni paripuṇṇaṁ parisuddhaṁ brahmacariyaṁ carati, ‘niddaso bhikkhū’ti alaṁvacanāyā”ti.

translation_text.an7.43:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Kosambī, in Ghosita’s Monastery.
translation_text.an7.43:1.3: Then Venerable Ānanda robed up in the morning and, taking his bowl and robe, entered Kosambī for alms.
translation_text.an7.43:1.5: “It’s too early to wander for alms in Kosambī.
translation_text.an7.43:1.6: Why don’t I visit the monastery of the wanderers of other religions?”
translation_text.an7.43:1.7: Then he went to the monastery of the wanderers of other religions and exchanged greetings with the wanderers there.
translation_text.an7.43:1.8: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side.
translation_text.an7.43:2.1: Now at that time while those wanderers of other religions were sitting together this discussion came up among them,
translation_text.an7.43:2.2: “Reverends, anyone who lives the full and pure spiritual life for twelve years is qualified to be called a ‘graduate mendicant’.”
translation_text.an7.43:3.1: Ānanda neither approved nor rejected that statement of the wanderers of other religions.
translation_text.an7.43:3.2: He got up from his seat, thinking,
translation_text.an7.43:3.3: “I will learn the meaning of this statement from the Buddha himself.”
translation_text.an7.43:3.4: Then Ānanda wandered for alms in Kosambī. After the meal, on his return from almsround, he went to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and told him what had happened, adding:
translation_text.an7.43:5.6: “Sir, in this teaching and training can we describe a mendicant as a ‘graduate’ solely because they have completed a certain number of years?”
translation_text.an7.43:6.2: These are the seven qualifications for graduation that I make known after realizing them with my own insight.
translation_text.an7.43:7.2: It’s when someone is faithful, conscientious, prudent, learned, energetic, mindful, and wise.
translation_text.an7.43:7.3: These are the seven qualifications for graduation that I make known after realizing them with my own insight.
translation_text.an7.43:7.4: A mendicant who possesses these seven qualifications for graduation is qualified to be called a ‘graduate mendicant’. This is so whether they have lived the full and pure spiritual life for twelve years,
